Title: Structures and mechanism of the AUX/LAX transporters involved in auxin import

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates high-affinity IAA import and also transports/recognizes synthetic auxin analogs 2,4-D; competitively inhibited by 1-NOA and 2-NOA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis LAX3 is the central experimentally characterized AUX/LAX transporter in this study. 2. Xenopus oocyte assays showed high-affinity IAA uptake with apparent Km about 218–228 nM and pH-dependent transport, maximal at acidic pH. 3. Cryo-EM structures captured apo, IAA-bound, 2,4-D-bound and 2-NOA-bound states, defining the ligand-binding pocket and conformational cycle. 4. Mutational analysis demonstrated that key binding-pocket residues and conserved histidines are essential for transport. 5. MD simulations support a proton-coupled auxin import mechanism with likely 1:1 H+:IAA stoichiometry.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AUX/LAX auxin influx carrier family; amino acid–polyamine–organocation (APC) superfamily transporter with 11 transmembrane helices
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Xenopus oocytes; radiolabeled uptake assays; competition assays; site-directed mutagenesis; cryo-EM structures; molecular dynamics simulations
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Background text states LAX3 is involved in lateral root development, vascular development and root hair formation; specific expression pattern not experimentally profiled in this study
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin directs plant growth and development, including tropic responses such as phototropism and gravitropism
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: External pH affected transport; highest IAA uptake at pH 5.0 and no uptake at pH 7.4; assays included IAA, 2,4-D, 1-NOA, 2-NOA, FCCP and 2,4-DNP treatments
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Structural knowledge of substrate recognition may enable future modification or modulation of hormone substrate recognition in AUX/LAX transporters
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper is mechanistic transporter biology focused on hormone transport rather than nutritional metabolite accumulation; AUX1, LAX1 and LAX2 are mentioned mainly as family/background context and are not core experimentally analyzed genes.
